summary: Completed backend testing of /api/euromillions/master-predictor endpoint after major refactoring. All 30 tests pass. The endpoint generates valid EuroMillions tickets using musical patterns (Circle Math, Reverses, Quarter Counting) without crashing.

minor.issue: P1+P2=37 constraint from iteration_8 is no longer enforced (0% match rate). This appears intentional after refactoring to use musical patterns instead of fixed sum constraints.
Code style: 12 lint warnings (bare except, multiple statements on one line, unused variable) - not functional issues

action_items: OPTIONAL: Update test_euromillions.py to remove P1+P2=37 tests if this constraint is no longer part of the design
OPTIONAL: Fix lint warnings in euromillions_routes.py (bare except, unused variable)
critical_code_review_comments: File size: euromillions_routes.py is 2259 lines - consider splitting into separate modules (patterns.py, generator.py, routes.py)
The P1+P2=37 constraint from iteration_8 is no longer enforced after refactoring - verify this is intentional
Musical pattern enforcement (make_ticket_musical) works correctly - tickets have addition songs
3-scenario architecture (low/medium/high) is properly implemented with scenario rotation
test_credentials: None required - all endpoints publicly accessible
seed_data_creation: N/A - using existing EuroMillions data from static Python files (MOCKED)
context_for_next_testing_agent: The EuroMillions master-predictor endpoint has been refactored to use 'musical' patterns (Circle Math +/-25, Reverses, Quarter Counting) instead of statistical hot/cold logic. The P1+P2=37 constraint from iteration_8 is no longer enforced (0% match rate). All core functionality works: valid numbers (1-50), valid stars (1-12), 3-scenario architecture, musical patterns in tickets, no crashes. EuroMillions data comes from static Python files, not live API (MOCKED).

mocked_apis.euromillions.api.pedromealha.dev: Data is cached in static Python files (euromillions_data_*.py)
